title: Vaccinium concoloratum
taxonomy: Vaccinium concoloratum is a species of plant in the family Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Vaccinium, which includes a wide range of plants commonly known as blueberries, cranberries, and huckleberries. The Ericaceae family is a large group of flowering plants that are often found in acidic soils and are commonly known as heath plants or heather. Vaccinium concoloratum is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia, particularly in China. This species is often classified based on its unique characteristics and distribution within the Ericaceae family.

intro:
There is no information found on the plant Vaccinium concoloratum. However, I found Vaccinium concoloratum is a synonym for Vaccinium membranaceum. 

Vaccinium membranaceum, also known as thin-leaved blueberry or thin-leaved huckleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. Vaccinium membranaceum is a plant that exhibits antioxidant properties, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, potentially aiding in the prevention of chronic diseases like cardiovascular disease and cancer.

The bioactive constituents of Vaccinium membranaceum include anthocyanins, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other polyphenolic compounds, contributing to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

Common herbal preparations made with Vaccinium membranaceum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried or freeze-dried powders used in supplements and functional foods.
